title: bryodesma muticum
side_effects: Bryodesma muticum can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory issues in some individuals. Ingesting the plant can cause gastrointestinal problems, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, as well as interact with certain medications. Long-term exposure to the plant's chemicals can lead to liver and kidney damage, and in severe cases, even organ failure. If ingested in large quantities, bryodesma muticum can also induce seizures and coma in rare instances.
